title: Rogue access points
description: Check your understanding of how rogue access points can intercept data in this set of free practice questions designed for AP Computer Science Principles students.
topic_path: Going deeper with recognizing and avoiding online scams

assessment_items.question_content:
Ayah wants to watch Khan Academy videos in the backyard, but there is no Internet connection in her yard. Since it rains frequently, Ayah doesn’t want to place a cable on the ground.  

**What device could Ayah install in order to access the Web in the backyard?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A wired router, because it interprets wired signals and routes packets through the Internet
A wireless router, because it uses an access point to interpret wireless signals and route packets through the Internet
A GPS device, because it can connect to satellites to provide Internet access
A Smart TV, because it can play videos from the Web

assessment_items.question_content:
Annalee is setting up a research lab. She wants to be able to search the Web from her laptop anywhere in the lab but doesn't want to drag an Ethernet cable around the lab.

Annalee starts browsing online for products.

**Which of the following products should she buy?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: NetGear Wireless Access Point
Maxone Portable Hard Drive
Quezqa Wireless Charging Pad
SharkTap Network Sniffer

assessment_items.question_content:
Sümeyye is buying a subscription from an Internet Service Provider (ISP) for her new apartment so that she can have wireless Internet access in each room.  She already has a laptop that can connect to WiFi networks. 

**Does Sümeyye need to purchase any additional hardware to have wireless Internet access?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: No, the laptop can connect wirelessly to the Internet as long as the apartment has a working wired connection to the Internet.
She only needs to purchase more hardware if she wants a fast wireless connection. She can connect wirelessly to a slow connection without additional hardware.
She only needs to purchase more hardware if she wants a secure wireless connection. She can connect wirelessly to an insecure connection without additional hardware.
Yes, she needs a wireless router that will send and receive packets from the apartment's wired Internet connection.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following could *not* occur if your computer is connected to the Internet over a rogue access point?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The rogue access point could read the files on your device.
The rogue access point could analyze the types of websites you visit.
The rogue access point could modify the contents of your connection to a website.
The rogue access point could see what keywords you're searching for on a web search engine.

assessment_items.question_content:
Femke went to a computer lab and connected her laptop to the WiFi network. She later received an email from the lab administrator warning that the WiFi network was in fact a rogue access point.

**Which of the following could have occurred while she was connected to the rogue access point?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: When she used her laptop to submit an online form, the rogue access point could have modified her form submission on its way to the server.
The rogue access point could have stolen all the passwords from her laptop's password manager application.
When she used the spreadsheets application on her laptop, the rogue access point could have seen all her saved spreadsheet files.
The rogue access point could have seen all the websites she visited in the previous month.

assessment_items.question_content:
An IT administrator for a company discovers that someone set up a rogue access point in the office and that several employees are connected to it. The administrator is concerned about an attacker gaining access to company data.

**Which of these attacks are possible over the rogue access point?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Intercepting requests sent over the Internet and copying their contents
Installing keylogging software on an employee's laptop
Logging into the company's server and stealing the database contents
Getting administrative access to an employee's laptop and stealing their files

assessment_items.question_content:
Joaquin needs to use online banking to transfer large amounts of money and is concerned about an attacker using a rogue access point to intercept his bank transfer.

**What's the best way for him to avoid the risks of a rogue access point?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Use a wired connection instead of a wireless connection.
Only connect to secured wireless connections.
Use multi-factor authentication when logging into the banking websites.
Use a strong password when logging into the banking websites.

assessment_items.question_content:
An investigative journalist connects to the Internet over a wired Ethernet connection in a government building. They don't want anyone else to see which websites they're visiting.

**Which entities might be able to see the websites visited in that browsing session?**

* $\text{I}$. A rogue access point
* $\text{II}$. The building's Internet Service Provider (ISP)
* $\text{III}$. Their computer's web browser

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: $\text{I}$ and $\text{II}$ only
$\text{II}$ and $\text{III}$ only
$\text{I}$ and $\text{III}$ only
$\text{I}$, $\text{II}$, and $\text{III}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Addison visits a café while visiting a friend in another city.
 The café offers three options for Internet connection:

* A public WiFi hotspot
* An Ethernet cable along the wall
* A password-protected WiFi hotspot (food purchase required)

**Which of those could be a rogue access point?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Only the public WiFi hotspot
Only the password-protected WiFi hotspot
Either of the WiFi hotspots
Any of them could be a rogue access point.

assessment_items.question_content:
Kamdyn is at a cafe and sees an unsecured network called "Free WiFi".

As soon as he connects to the network, someone near him warns him that it's probably a rogue access point. He's concerned now about an attacker having seen his Internet requests.

**Which of his Internet requests could have been seen by the rogue access point?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The rogue access point could have seen all the Internet requests sent by his laptop after the connection was made.
The rogue access point could have seen all the Internet requests sent by his laptop since its most recent shutdown.
The rogue access point could have seen all the Internet requests that have ever been sent by his laptop.
The rogue access point could have seen all the Internet requests sent by his laptop over wireless access points (but not wired).

assessment_items.question_content:
At 9:30 AM, Thato went to a local coworking space and connected to the usual WiFi network ("COWIFI") and started browsing the morning news. At 10 AM, he noticed that webpages were slow to load and connected to a different free network ("COFFEE-FI") instead. However, a coworker walked by at 10:25 and warned him that the "COFFEE-FI" network was actually a rogue access point, so he disconnected from it and reconnected to "COWIFI".

Here is his browsing history from that morning:

Domain | Time 
- | - | -
cnn.com | 9:42
theverge.com | 9:56
desmoinesregister.com | 10:07
sltrib.com | 10:22
espn.com | 10:28
bbc.com | 10:34

**Which requests would the rogue access point have seen?**

👁️**Note that there are 2 answers to this question.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: cnn.com @ 9:42
theverge.com @ 9:56
desmoinesregister.com @ 10:07
sltrib.com @ 10:22
espn.com @ 10:28
bbc.com @ 10:34

assessment_items.question_content:
Emilia arrived to work at her company's office at 9 AM. 
 She connected her laptop to the WiFi hotspot labeled "OfficeWiFi5thFl". After a meeting ended at 10:30 AM, she connected to a different hotspot labeled "OfficeSpace5thFl". After lunch ended at 12 PM, her laptop lost that signal and reconnected to "OfficeWiFi5thFl". 

**If "OfficeSpace5thFl" was actually a rogue access point, which website visits could it have intercepted?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A visit to an analytics website at 9:20 AM where she downloaded the weekly analytics data.
A visit to an online spreadsheets website at 9:50 where she filled out several rows of information about internal team deadlines.
A visit to a tax software website at 10:45 AM where she filled out part of her tax form for this year.
A visit to an expense management website at 12:15 PM where she submitted a receipt for reimbursement.
All of the visits described above.
